channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [ZENITHEXPO] Zenith Exports clarifies price movement, no undisclosed events
item.description:
Zenith Exports Ltd received a request from BSE seeking clarification on significant price movement of its securities. In response, the company stated that as of today, there are no events or information required to be disclosed under Regulation 30 of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, that would have a bearing on its operations or performance. The company also confirmed that there are no major significant changes in its business and affairs, attributing the price movement to market forces. Zenith Exports reiterated its commitment to timely disclosure of all necessary information to the stock exchanges.

  • BSE sought clarification on significant price movement of company's securities.
  • Company states no undisclosed events or information affecting operations/performance.
  • No major changes in business or affairs as of the current date.
  • Price movement is entirely market-based, according to the company.
  • Company commits to timely disclosures under SEBI (LODR) Regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ZENITH EXPORTS LTD. | Category: Clarification

item.title: [SKRABUL] Shukra Bullions confirms no public issue proceeds raised in FY26
item.description:
Shukra Bullions Ltd has confirmed that it did not raise any public issue proceeds from Initial Public Offer (IPO), Further Public Offer (FPO), Rights Issue, or Preferential Issue during the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. As a result, the statement of deviation(s) or variation(s) under Regulation 32 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, is not applicable to the company for this reporting period. This filing ensures compliance with regulatory disclosure requirements.

  • Shukra Bullions confirms no public issue proceeds raised in FY26.
  • No funds raised via IPO, FPO, Rights Issue, or Preferential Issue.
  • Regulation 32 deviation statement is not applicable to the company.
  • Filing covers the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SHUKRA BULLIONS LTD. | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [EVEXIA] Evexia Lifecare Appoints Tejas K. Soni & Co. as Statutory Auditor
item.description:
Evexia Lifecare Ltd. has announced the appointment of M/s. Tejas K. Soni and Company, Chartered Accountants (FRN: 135093W), as its Statutory Auditor. This appointment, approved by the Board of Directors on February 17, 2026, is to fill a casual vacancy for the Financial Year 2025-26, arising from the resignation of the previous auditor. The company acknowledged a delay in disclosing this appointment, attributing it to an inadvertent administrative oversight, and expressed regret while assuring improved internal compliance mechanisms. M/s. Tejas K. Soni and Company, a Vadodara-based firm, is Peer Reviewed by the ICAI and has no association with the company's directors.

  • Evexia Lifecare appointed Tejas K. Soni & Co. as Statutory Auditor.
  • The appointment fills a casual vacancy for FY 2025-26.
  • Board approved the auditor on February 17, 2026.
  • Company cited administrative oversight for delayed disclosure.
  • New auditor is an independent, peer-reviewed firm.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Evexia Lifecare Ltd | Category: Auditor

item.title: [EVEXIA] Evexia Lifecare: Auditor M. A. Shah & Co. Resigns; Delay in Disclosure
item.description:
Evexia Lifecare Ltd announced the resignation of its statutory auditor, M/s M. A. Shah & Co., Chartered Accountants, effective February 12, 2026. The company acknowledged an inadvertent delay in submitting this disclosure, attributing it to internal administrative oversight and human error, and expressed regret for the lapse. M. A. Shah & Co. stated that their appointment was to fill a casual vacancy for FY 2024-25, with their tenure linked to the conclusion of the Annual General Meeting (AGM) for that fiscal year. As the AGM was not held within the stipulated or extended time, the auditors conveyed their unwillingness to continue beyond the appointed term. The last report submitted by the auditor was a limited review report for Q2 FY 2025-26, dated November 14, 2025. Evexia Lifecare assured that steps are being taken to strengthen internal processes for timely compliance.

  • Statutory auditor M. A. Shah & Co. resigned from Evexia Lifecare.
  • Resignation was effective February 12, 2026, with reporting delay.
  • Auditor cited delayed AGM for FY24-25 as reason for non-continuation.
  • Company attributes delay to inadvertent human error and oversight.
  • Evexia Lifecare is strengthening internal compliance processes.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Evexia Lifecare Ltd | Category: Auditor

item.title: [COMFINCAP] Comfort Fincap converts 80.50 lakh warrants into equity shares
item.description:
Comfort Fincap Ltd. announced the conversion of 80,50,000 share warrants into an equal number of equity shares of face value ₹2 each through a preferential allotment. The Board of Directors approved this on April 17, 2026. The company received ₹5,43,37,500, representing the remaining 75% of the issue price (₹6.75 per warrant). These newly allotted equity shares will rank pari-passu with existing shares. Consequently, the company's paid-up equity capital increased from ₹18,00,13,000 (9,00,06,500 equity shares) to ₹19,61,13,000 (9,80,56,500 equity shares). The total issue price per warrant was ₹9.

  • 80.50 lakh share warrants converted into equity shares.
  • Conversion completed via preferential allotment.
  • Company received ₹5.43 Cr as balance payment.
  • Paid-up equity capital increased to ₹19.61 Cr.
  • New shares rank pari-passu with existing equity shares.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: COMFORT FINCAP LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [COMFINCAP] Comfort Fincap converts 80.50 lakh warrants to equity; capital up by ₹1.61 Cr
item.description:
Comfort Fincap Ltd's Board of Directors approved the conversion of 80,50,000 share warrants into an equal number of equity shares of face value ₹2 each via preferential allotment on April 17, 2026. The company received ₹5,43,37,500, representing the remaining 75% of the issue price at ₹6.75 per warrant. Consequent to this conversion, the paid-up equity capital of the company has increased from ₹18,00,13,000 (9,00,06,500 shares) to ₹19,61,13,000 (9,80,56,500 shares). The newly allotted equity shares will rank pari-passu with the existing equity shares.

  • Board approved conversion of 80.50 lakh share warrants into equity shares.
  • Equity shares were allotted via preferential allotment to four investors.
  • Company received ₹5.43 crore as the remaining 75% payment for warrants.
  • Paid-up equity capital increased by ₹1.61 crore to ₹19.61 crore.
  • New equity shares rank pari-passu with existing shares.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: COMFORT FINCAP LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [NIITMTS] NIIT MTS Ranked Top 20 Staffing & Temporary Resources Companies 2026
item.description:
NIIT Learning Systems Limited (NIIT MTS), a global leader in managed learning services, announced its inclusion in Training Industry, Inc.'s 2026 Top 20 Companies in Staffing and Temporary Resources. This recognition by the leading research and information resource for corporate learning leaders is based on criteria such as the scope and quality of staffing roles, market presence, client portfolio strength, and business performance. NIIT MTS highlighted its shift towards integrated, outcome-driven talent strategies and a managed services model, emphasizing its commitment to helping clients build agile, future-ready capabilities and deliver measurable impact in learning and development.

  • NIIT MTS recognized in Training Industry's Top 20 Staffing & Temporary Resources for 2026.
  • Selection based on scope, market presence, client portfolio, and growth trajectory.
  • Highlights NIIT's shift to outcome-driven talent strategies and managed services model.
  • Reinforces NIIT MTS's position as a global leader in managed learning services.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: NIIT Learning Systems Ltd | Category: Award

item.title: [SWANENERGY] Swan Corp clarifies volume increase; no undisclosed info
item.description:
Swan Corp Ltd responded to a BSE letter regarding a significant increase in its trading volume. The company clarified that it has made all necessary disclosures under Regulation 30 of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015. It stated that no material information or event has been withheld that could impact the scrip's price or volume behavior. All information provided by the company is already in the public domain, and Swan Corp will continue to comply with SEBI regulations, ensuring timely disclosure of all price-sensitive information to the stock exchanges.

  • Swan Corp clarified unusual volume increase.
  • Company confirmed compliance with SEBI (LODR) Regulations.
  • No undisclosed material information affecting scrip.
  • All relevant information is in the public domain.
  • Company will continue timely disclosures to exchanges.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SWAN ENERGY LTD. | Category: Clarification

item.title: [ZENTEC] Zen Technologies receives Arms Manufacturing Licence for Cannons
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Zen Technologies Ltd has been granted an arms manufacturing licence by the Government of India under the Arms Act, 1959. This licence authorizes the company to manufacture 12.7mm, 23mm, 30mm, and 40mm cannons. These critical weapon systems are applicable in Air Defence, naval operations, and Counter-Unmanned Aircraft System (C-UAS) roles, providing effective last-layer defence against drones and other aerial threats. The move reinforces Zen Technologies' leadership in Air Defence and Counter-Drone capabilities, marking a significant step in advancing India’s indigenous defence manufacturing ecosystem under the IDDM framework.

  • Zen Technologies secured an arms manufacturing license from the Indian government.
  • The license permits production of 12.7mm, 23mm, 30mm, and 40mm cannons.
  • These cannons are crucial for Air Defence and Counter-Drone (C-UAS) capabilities.
  • It strengthens India's indigenous defence manufacturing ecosystem under IDDM.
  • The systems offer responsive and cost-effective protection against aerial threats.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ZEN TECHNOLOGIES LTD. | Category: Business Update

item.title: [ASHAPURMIN] Ashapura Minechem clarifies on increased share volume, no pending disclosures
item.description:
Ashapura Minechem Ltd responded to an email from BSE dated April 17, 2026, seeking clarification regarding an increase in the company's share trading volume. The company stated that it has fully complied with all material disclosure requirements under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015. Ashapura Minechem confirmed that there is no information or announcement pending that would trigger a disclosure under Regulation 30, to the knowledge of its management. The company reiterated its commitment to good Corporate Governance and assured that it will continue to abide by all applicable rules and regulations, including keeping stock exchanges informed of price-sensitive information.

  • BSE sought clarification from Ashapura Minechem on increased share trading volume.
  • Company confirmed compliance with SEBI (LODR) Regulation 30 disclosure norms.
  • Management stated no undisclosed price-sensitive information is pending.
  • Ashapura Minechem reaffirmed its commitment to good Corporate Governance.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ASHAPURA MINECHEM LTD. | Category: Clarification
